reasoning: The 1-year Treasury yield dropped from ~3.55% in early Nov down to 3.38% on Dec 19 in anticipation of and following the Fed's 25bp rate cut on Dec 10. The Fed signaled a "hawkish" stance, implying a pause and potentially only one cut next year. Since 3.38% appropriately prices the expectations of 1 rate cut over the next year (relative to current 3.5-3.75% fed funds), the fast downward trend should stabilize. The market has found a floor around 3.36%-3.38%. An upward bounce due to mean reversion or seasonal factors is slightly more likely in the short term. Over longer horizons, the forecast approaches a 50/50 probability, making it closer to 0.5.
